name: VBR — Version-Based Reclamation
summary: Sheffi, Herlihy, Petrank (DISC 2021) use fully optimistic reclamation — both reads and writes are speculative. Each object carries a version stamp; operations validate version on read and CAS-with-version on write, abort/retry on mismatch. Eliminates hazard-pointer protection on writes; reuses freed memory immediately into a typed allocator. Outperforms EBR/HP/HE/IBR on linked-list, hash-table, skip-list benchmarks while remaining lock-free.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Sheffi, Herlihy, Petrank's **Version-Based Reclamation** (DISC 2021) uses *fully optimistic* reclamation — both reads *and* writes are speculative. Each object carries a version stamp; operations validate the version on read and use CAS-with-version on write, aborting and retrying on mismatch.

The key advance: prior optimistic schemes only optimised reads, still requiring HP-style protection on writes. VBR eliminates hazard-pointer protection on writes too, by tying every write's CAS to a version that detects the use-after-free race directly. Freed memory is **reused immediately into a typed allocator** — there is no quarantine period because a stale operation will simply observe a version mismatch on its next CAS and retry.

Outperforms EBR/HP/HE/IBR on linked-list, hash-table, and skip-list benchmarks while remaining lock-free.
